nameMeanings.name: Hutchinson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): patronymic from the Old French personal name Hucheon (see Houchin ) + -son.
Irish: in Ireland and Scotland, adopted for Mac Uisdein and Mac Uistin; see McCutcheon .

History: Anne Marbury Hutchinson (1591–1643) and her husband William came from Lincolnshire, England, to MA in 1634. A religious dissident, she led the first attack on the Puritans and was banished from the Massachusetts Bay Colony. She moved to RI in 1638, and, after her husband's death in 1642, settled in NY, where the Hutchinson River was named in her honor. Ironically, the Hutchinson name stayed on in MA and one of her descendants, Thomas Hutchinson (1711–80), was royal governor of Massachusetts Bay Colony.

variants.name: Houchin
meanings: English (Essex): of Norman origin, from the Old French personal name Huchon, a double diminutive of Hue (see Hugh ) common in Picardy, corresponding to Old French Hueçon, Huesson, modern French Husson. This was used as a personal name in Scotland, where it also became a surname and was adopted in Gaelic as Huisdean or Uisdean; see McCutcheon and compare Hutch .

nameMeanings.name: Barnard
meanings: English: An English form of Bernard.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Barnard Hutchinson Esquire was born before 1282, in Cowlam, Yorkshire, England, his father, John Hutchinson, was 32 and his mother, Edith Wouldbie, was 32. He had at least 4 sons and 6 daughters with Mary Beatrice de Boyville. He died on 8 October 1362, in his hometown, and was buried in Doncaster, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Barnard Hutchinson Esquire est né avant 1282, à Cowlam, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, John Hutchinson, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Edith Wouldbie, avait 32 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles avec Mary Beatrice de Boyville. Il est décédé le 8 octobre 1362, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Doncaster, Everwyck, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Barnard Hutchinson Esquire(이)가 1282년 이전 영국 요르크실르 코우램에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Hutchinson(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Edith Wouldbie(은)는 32세였다. 그는 Mary Beatrice de Boyville(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1362년 10월 8일 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 돈카스터에 묻혔다.
